⭐ Health and Physical Education

Yoga

Discover the ancient practice of yoga for body, mind, and soul!

📚 Unit 9 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 9: Yoga

Yoga is an ancient practice from India that connects the body, mind, and breath. It includes physical postures (asanas), breathing exercises (pranayama), and meditation (dhyana).

Lesson 1: Yoga, Meditation and Pranayam

What is Yoga?

Yoga comes from the Sanskrit word “yuj” meaning “to join” or “to unite”. It unites the body, mind, and spirit.

Benefits of Yoga

BenefitExplanation
Improves flexibilityStretches muscles
Builds strengthHolds postures
Better postureAligns the spine
Reduces stressCalms the mind
Improves concentrationFocus on breath and body
Better breathingPranayama cleanses lungs
Healthy digestionMassages internal organs
Peaceful sleepRelaxes the body

Basic Principles of Yoga

PrincipleMeaning
Regular practiceDo yoga daily (even 15-20 minutes)
Empty stomachPractice 2-3 hours after eating
Quiet placeClean, calm, well-ventilated area
Proper clothingLoose, comfortable clothes
BarefootBetter grip and grounding
Listen to your bodyDo not force or strain

Yoga Asanas (Postures) for Class 5

1. Tadasana (Mountain Pose)

StepAction
1Stand with feet together
2Keep spine straight
3Arms at sides, palms facing forward
4Distribute weight evenly on both feet
5Breathe normally

Benefits: Improves posture, strengthens thighs and ankles.

2. Vrikshasana (Tree Pose)

StepAction
1Start in Tadasana
2Bend right knee, place right foot on left thigh
3Balance on left foot
4Bring palms together at chest (prayer position)
5Raise arms above head
6Hold for 30 seconds, repeat with other leg

Benefits: Improves balance, strengthens legs, increases concentration.

💡 Tip: Focus on a fixed point to balance better.

3. Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose)

StepAction
1Lie face down, legs together
2Place palms under shoulders
3Inhale, lift chest and head
4Keep navel on the floor
5Look up, shoulders away from ears
6Hold for 15-30 seconds, exhale down

Benefits: Strengthens spine, opens chest, improves posture.

4. Marjaryasana-Bitilasana (Cat-Cow Stretch)

StepAction
1Come on hands and knees (table position)
2Inhale, drop belly, lift head and tailbone (Cow)
3Exhale, round spine, tuck chin (Cat)
4Repeat 5-10 times with breath

Benefits: Warms up the spine, stretches back and neck.

5. Balasana (Child’s Pose)

StepAction
1Kneel on the floor
2Sit back on heels
3Fold forward, place forehead on floor
4Stretch arms forward or rest by sides
5Breathe deeply, hold for 1-2 minutes

Benefits: Restful pose, releases back tension, calms the mind.

6. Setu Bandhasana (Bridge Pose)

StepAction
1Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat
2Place arms by sides, palms down
3Inhale, lift hips toward ceiling
4Clasp hands under the back
5Hold for 30 seconds, exhale down

Benefits: Strengthens back and glutes, opens chest.

7. Shavasana (Corpse Pose - Final Relaxation)

StepAction
1Lie flat on back
2Legs slightly apart, arms by sides (palms up)
3Close eyes
4Breathe naturally
5Relax each body part from toes to head
6Stay for 5-10 minutes

Benefits: Deep relaxation, reduces stress, integrates practice.

💡 Shavasana is the most important pose! Never skip final relaxation.

Surya Namaskar (Sun Salutation)

Surya Namaskar is a sequence of 12 postures done in a flowing movement. It is a complete exercise for the whole body.

StepPoseBreath
1Pranamasana (Prayer pose)Exhale
2Hasta Uttanasana (Raised arms)Inhale
3Padahastasana (Hand to foot)Exhale
4Ashwa Sanchalanasana (Equestrian)Inhale
5Parvatasana (Mountain)Exhale
6Ashtanga Namaskara (Eight limbs)Hold
7Bhujangasana (Cobra)Inhale
8Parvatasana (Mountain)Exhale
9Ashwa Sanchalanasana (Equestrian)Inhale
10Padahastasana (Hand to foot)Exhale
11Hasta Uttanasana (Raised arms)Inhale
12Pranamasana (Prayer pose)Exhale

Benefits of Surya Namaskar:

  • Exercises all major muscle groups
  • Improves blood circulation
  • Strengthens heart and lungs
  • Helps with weight control
  • Takes only 5-10 minutes

Pranayama (Breathing Exercises)

Pranayama = Prana (life energy) + Yama (control). It means controlling the breath.

1. Deep Breathing (Dheerga Swasam)

StepAction
1Sit comfortably
2Inhale slowly through nose for 4 counts
3Feel belly and chest expand
4Exhale slowly through nose for 4 counts
5Repeat 5-10 times

Benefits: Calms the mind, increases oxygen.

2. Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril Breathing)

StepAction
1Sit comfortably
2Close right nostril with thumb
3Inhale through left nostril
4Close left nostril with ring finger
5Exhale through right nostril
6Inhale through right nostril
7Close right nostril, exhale through left
8Repeat for 5-10 minutes

Benefits: Balances left and right brain, reduces stress.

3. Bhramari (Humming Bee Breath)

StepAction
1Sit comfortably, close eyes
2Place index fingers on ears (cartilage)
3Take a deep breath in
4Exhale slowly making a humming sound like a bee (mmmm)
5Repeat 5 times

Benefits: Calms the mind, reduces anxiety, helps with sleep.

4. Kapalbhati (Skull Shining Breath)

StepAction
1Sit comfortably
2Take a deep breath in
3Exhale forcefully through nose (pump belly in)
4Inhalation happens automatically
5Do 20-30 pumps (count)
6Rest, then repeat

Benefits: Cleanses lungs, strengthens diaphragm, improves digestion.

⚠️ Note: Do not do Kapalbhati if you have high blood pressure, heart problems, or are pregnant.

Meditation (Dhyana)

Meditation is focusing the mind on one thing (like breath, a sound, or an image) to achieve mental clarity and calmness.

How to Meditate (Simple Steps)

StepAction
1Sit in a comfortable position
2Close eyes gently
3Bring attention to natural breath
4When mind wanders (it will!), gently bring it back
5Start with 2-5 minutes, gradually increase

Benefits of Meditation for Children

BenefitExplanation
Better focusCan concentrate in class
Less anxietyFeels calm and peaceful
Better sleepFall asleep faster
Emotional controlLess anger and frustration
Self-awarenessUnderstand feelings better

Simple Meditation Techniques for Children

TechniqueHow to do it
Breath awarenessCount breaths: inhale (1), exhale (2)… up to 10, then restart
Body scanSlowly move attention from toes to head, relaxing each part
Loving-kindnessThink “May I be happy. May my friend be happy. May all be happy”
Candle gazingLook at a candle flame (with adult supervision) then close eyes

Yoga for Specific Purposes

PurposeRecommended Asanas
Better concentrationVrikshasana, Tadasana, meditation
Good digestionBhujangasana, Pavanamuktasana (wind-relieving pose)
Strong backSetu Bandhasana, Cat-Cow
RelaxationBalasana, Shavasana
Energy boostSurya Namaskar, Kapalbhati

Yoga Do’s and Don’ts

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Practice on an empty stomachEat just before yoga
Wear comfortable clothesWear tight or restrictive clothes
Use a yoga mat or clean floorPractice on uneven or hard surface
Breathe through the noseHold your breath
Stop if you feel painForce your body into a pose
Practice regularlyDo yoga only once a month
End with ShavasanaSkip final relaxation

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The word “yoga” comes from the Sanskrit word ______.
  2. The final relaxation pose is called ______.
  3. ______ is the practice of controlling the breath.
  4. Anulom Vilom is a type of ______.
  5. ______ pose is also known as the Tree Pose.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Yoga should be done on a full stomach. (T/F)
  2. Shavasana is the most important yoga pose. (T/F)
  3. Pranayama means breathing exercises. (T/F)
  4. Kapalbhati is a calming, slow breath. (T/F)
  5. Meditation helps improve concentration. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Pose to Its Name

DescriptionPose Name
Standing like a mountaina) Balasana
Child’s pose (resting)b) Vrikshasana
Tree pose (balancing)c) Tadasana
Cobra pose (backbend)d) Bhujangasana

Exercise 4: Name the Pranayama

  1. Alternate nostril breathing → ______
  2. Humming like a bee → ______
  3. Forceful exhalations (pumping belly) → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. yuj, 2. Shavasana, 3. Pranayama, 4. pranayama, 5. Vrikshasana

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. T, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Tadasana→c, Balasana→a, Vrikshasana→b, Bhujangasana→d

Exercise 4: 1. Anulom Vilom, 2. Bhramari, 3. Kapalbhati

Great work! Yoga is a gift for life. Practice daily for a healthy body and peaceful mind!
